title: Food Stamps/SNAP * Domestic Violence Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Food > Food Stamps/SNAP * Domestic Violence Issues 1 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Domestic Violence Issues Older Adults Veterans No Filter Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES SELF-SUFFICIENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Oregon Department of Human Services (ODHS) provides services for survivors of domestic violence with Self-Sufficiency programs, including determining eligibility for a temporary assistance grant and SNAP. Grants may be provided on a same-day basis in an emergency. 10305 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: A person fleeing domestic violence, intimate partner violence, or a person trying to stay safe whose safety is at risk because of domestic violence or the threat of domestic violence. The person must also fit the following criteria: -Be a pregnant woman or a parent or relative caring for a minor child -Be a resident of Oregon -Meet the income criteria for the program. This program looks only at income on hand that is available to meet any emergency needs. -Eligibility varies and is determined on a case-by-case basis Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 988-6400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES SELF-SUFFICIENCY SERVICES
